title: Repo bootstrap from PennyLane demo
what: Created standalone trainable-qsp-angles repo from qsp-pennylane-demo reference; copied circuit, tests, demo.ipynb, manuscript.
why: Paper and research need a self-contained home separate from community demo framing.
action: Populated root; added LICENSE/NOTICE (Ross Peili + ARPA Hellenic Logical Systems); pushed to github.com/rosspeili/trainable-qsp-angles.
rationale: Standalone repo aligns code, paper, and reproducibility with research claims rather than tutorial demo.

title: PennyLane root-finding angle solver fails on T1
what: qml.poly_to_angles(..., angle_solver='root-finding') raised on degree-5 sin Chebyshev target.
why: Root-finding solver is numerically fragile for this polynomial instance (known PennyLane behavior).
action: Default analytic baseline uses angle_solver='iterative'; document root-finding as optional.
rationale: Iterative solver succeeds reliably in PennyLane 0.44+ for our benchmark.

title: PennyLane analytic phases high MSE on flat circuit
what: PennyLane iterative poly_to_angles achieves train_mse ~ 0.59 on flat <X> circuit vs gradient train_mse ~ 9.6e-5 (seed 0, d=5).
why: Likely QSP convention mismatch: PennyLane angle convention vs flat RZ(-2phi) + H-RZ(arccos)-H oracle sequence.
action: Document convention_note in baseline.py; do not treat high MSE as solver failure; keep both metrics for paper.
rationale: Fair comparison requires reporting flat-circuit MSE (training target) separately from solver-native accuracy.

title: High-level PennyLane QSVT breaks JAX gradients
what: qml.QSVT template path yields zero or broken jax.grad through phase parameters.
why: Template captures concrete NumPy values at construction time; parameters leave autodiff graph.
action: Use flat inline gates in qsp_jax/circuit.py; add tests/test_jax_traceability.py documenting failure mode.
rationale: Gradient-based learning is core claim; circuit must keep phases as JAX tracers.

title: Even polynomial degrees invalid for sin target
what: Scaling/ablation with even degrees (e.g. 10, 20) fails parity constraints for odd sin approximation.
why: sin(x) on [-1,1] requires odd Chebyshev/monomial structure for QSP-admissible targets.
action: Protocol uses odd degrees only: [5, 7, 9, 15, 21] in experiments/configs/default.json.
rationale: Parity is a mathematical constraint, not a training hyperparameter.

title: Framework-agnostic framing; PennyLane as reference only
what: Documentation and manuscript softened to present PennyLane as reference implementation, not exclusive stack.
why: Research question is trainable QSP angles, not PennyLane advocacy; reviewers flagged SDK coupling.
action: Added docs/FRAMEWORKS.md; updated README and manuscript abstract/contributions.
rationale: Method transfers to Qiskit/Cirq/TFQ if flat primitives + autodiff are preserved.

title: Phase 2 experiment pipeline implemented
what: Added experiments/train.py, sweep.py (multi-seed, scaling, ablation), summarize.py, config protocol, notebooks.
why: Paper needs reproducible sweeps and comparison tables beyond single-seed demo.
action: Run via py -3.13 -m experiments.*; results gitignored except schema.json.
rationale: Module invocation avoids PYTHONPATH issues; JSON schema enables manuscript figure regeneration.

title: Fast + slow pytest split
what: 16 fast tests + 2 slow (@pytest.mark.slow) for full 500-step train; CI runs fast only.
why: Full training in CI would be too slow; still need regression for sub-mill MSE claim.
action: pytest.ini marks slow; .github/workflows/ci.yml uses -m 'not slow'.
rationale: Balance developer feedback speed with coverage of training convergence.

title: pyqsp CompletionError on raw monomial coefficients
what: QuantumSignalProcessingPhases(monomial_coeffs_numpy(5)) raised CompletionError during Chao exploration.
why: pyqsp expects Chebyshev-basis input (or Laurent form), not low-to-high monomial coeffs from PennyLane convention.
action: Added chebyshev_sin_cheb_coeffs(); pass Chebyshev array to pyqsp; use laurent method with chebyshev_basis=False.
rationale: Match pyqsp API per angle_sequence.py and test_response.py examples.

title: pyqsp sym_qsp without chebyshev_basis flag
what: QuantumSignalProcessingPhases(..., method='sym_qsp') raised ValueError requiring chebyshev_basis=True.
why: sym_qsp Newton solver operates on reduced Chebyshev coefficients with parity check.
action: Set chebyshev_basis=True for sym_qsp; kept laurent as default Chao path.
rationale: Laurent completion is the canonical Chao et al. method; sym_qsp is optional alternate.

title: pyqsp Wx/z completion fails on monomial P-type coeffs
what: QuantumSignalProcessingPhases(..., signal_operator='Wx', measurement='z') failed: Input P could not be completed.
why: Wx/z model expects monomial P-type coefficients converted from Chebyshev; wrong model for our setup.
action: Default Chao baseline uses Wx/x (or Wz/z) model; do not use Wx/z for T1.
rationale: Wx/x and Wz/z both succeed and give identical phases for our target in testing.

title: Chao baseline appended alongside PennyLane
what: Added qsp_jax/chao_baseline.py, tests/test_chao_baseline.py; extended summarize/sweep/baseline_analytic for dual baselines.
why: Need SDK-independent analytic comparison; user requested append-not-replace policy.
action: Third CSV row for Chao; scaling columns chao_* added next to analytic_* (PennyLane).
rationale: Side-by-side baselines support honest paper table: gradient vs PL vs Chao.

title: Chao native accuracy good; flat-circuit MSE still high
what: pyqsp Laurent d=5: pyqsp_reconstruction_max_error ≈ 1.32e-4; flat-circuit train_mse ≈ 0.951 (vs gradient ≈ 9.6e-5).
why: pyqsp uses exp(iφ) diagonal rotations and |+> measurement convention; our circuit uses RZ(-2φ) and <X>.
action: Report both metrics; convention_note on ChaoAnalyticResult; open issue for explicit phase mapping layer.
rationale: Solver succeeds in its convention; flat MSE is the training-relevant metric until mapping is derived.

title: sym_qsp verification used wrong response component
what: test_analytic_phases_chao_sym_qsp_shape failed: recon_err ≈ 1.39 using real(Wx/x) response.
why: sym_qsp targets imaginary part of top-left matrix element in sym_qsp convention.
action: verify_pyqsp_reconstruction(..., method='sym_qsp') compares np.imag(response) with sym_qsp=True.
rationale: Native verification must match each pyqsp method's documented convention.

title: Chao baseline test suite passes
what: 5 tests in test_chao_baseline.py pass: Chebyshev coeffs, laurent/sym_qsp shapes, metrics, verification consistency.
why: Prevent regressions in standalone analytic path.
action: Included in default fast pytest run.
rationale: Chao path is now part of core reproducibility surface.

title: Audit trail system added
what: Created docs/AUDIT_TRAIL.md, docs/audit/LOG.jsonl, qsp_jax/audit.py, experiments/audit.py CLI.
why: User requested labeled failure/fix log with rationale for all work including dead ends.
action: Backfilled JSONL from project history; linked from README and RESEARCH_PLAN.
rationale: Evidence density requires interpretive layer beyond raw results/ JSON.

title: Convention map pi/2 - phi_chao for flat circuit
what: Derived phi_flat = pi/2 - phi_chao; mapped d=5 MSE drops from ~0.95 to ~4.7e-3 on flat circuit.
why: pyqsp Wx/x uses exp(i phi) rotations; training circuit uses RZ(-2 phi) and <X>.
action: Added qsp_jax/convention.py and docs/CONVENTIONS.md; metrics report mapped + unmapped.
rationale: Enables apples-to-apples comparison with gradient training metric.

title: Direct PL-to-pyqsp inversion fails at d>=15
what: Endpoint pi/4 mapping worked at d=5 but inner PL+Chao mismatch max ~0.63 at d=15.
why: PennyLane sparse angle structure differs from pyqsp at high degree despite same Chebyshev target.
action: PennyLane mapped metrics use shared chao_to_flat(pyqsp(...)); PL angles_solver + metrics_unmapped kept for audit.
rationale: Same polynomial target; pyqsp bridge is stable across degrees in scaling table.

title: Phase 2 full experiment batch completed
what: Ran summarize baseline, sweep all (30-seed multi-seed, scaling d=5..21, ablation grid).
why: User requested immediate Phase 2 data before Phase 3 paper discussion.
action: Results under results/t1_degree5/, results/scaling/, results/ablation/, results/paper/.
rationale: Statistical and scaling evidence for manuscript Results section.

title: Phase 3 manuscript draft updated
what: manuscript.tex updated with data-driven figures, baseline table, 30-seed/scaling/baseline plots, Limitations, short Outlook; Zenodo DOI 10.5281/zenodo.20645403.
why: Paper must reflect Phase 2 evidence not old demo numbers.
action: Added experiments/export_manuscript_figures.py and committed manuscript_data/; user review before final deposit.
rationale: Mapped MSE in main table with unmapped in caption footnote; real circuit eval for poly figure with residual panel.

title: Manuscript v1.1 item 1: benchmark reframing
what: Reframed paper as reproducible benchmark + JAX implementation note; explicit scope that this is not a new QSP algorithm. Updated subtitle, abstract, C1-C4, Discussion opener, Outlook; README aligned.
why: Review feedback: align claims with deliverables (measurement, convention protocol, open artifacts) rather than paradigm language.
action: Edited manuscript.tex and README; arpa_logo.png on title page (local gitignored asset, paper/Zenodo only).
rationale: Honest positioning for v1.1; preserves all numerical results and code paths unchanged.

title: v1.1 item 2: multi-seed at d=7 and d=15
what: Ran 30-seed gradient training at d=7 and d=15 via sweep multi-seed --degrees 7,15. All 60 runs below 10^-3 train MSE. Added paper section 4.2.1, additive manuscript_numbers macros, golden test for legacy figures.
why: Limitations flagged single-seed scaling; friend review requested multi-seed evidence at higher degree without rerunning v1.0 figure sources.
action: New results/t1_degree7/ and t1_degree15/ only; extended sweep.py --degrees; tests/test_manuscript_numbers.py locks LossCurve/MultiSeed/Scaling/Poly macros.
rationale: Additive protocol preserves published v1.0 figure numbers while closing statistical gap at d=7,15.

title: v1.1 item 3: Chao sym_qsp vs Laurent audit
what: Compared pyqsp sym_qsp (machine-precision native recon) vs default Laurent at d=5. Mapped flat train MSE identical (~4.7e-3); sym_qsp native error ~1e-15 vs Laurent ~1.3e-4.
why: Review asked whether sym_qsp closes analytic gap or whether Laurent caveat must be explicit.
action: Added experiments/compare_chao_methods.py; updated manuscript baseline prose, Limitations, CONVENTIONS.md; main table unchanged (Laurent row).
rationale: Best of both: document machine-precision native solver + honest mapping residual; no misleading table change.

title: v1.1 item 4: off-grid random max error
what: 1024 uniform random x in [-0.95,0.95] excluding 64-point train grid; max pointwise error at d=5 seed 0. Learned 2.9e-2; mapped analytic 1.83e-1.
why: Review requested generalization beyond discrete training grid without changing headline MSE figures.
action: Added experiments/offgrid_eval.py reusing baseline_comparison_d5.json phases; paper section 4.5 + table; additive Offgrid* macros in manuscript_numbers.tex.
rationale: Additive eval confirms gradient advantage on off-grid probe; legacy figure macros unchanged.

title: v1.1 item 5: barren plateaus discussion
what: Added Discussion section 5.3 on barren plateaus vs QSP trainability; cites McClean 2018 and Cerezo 2021; ties to multi-seed success and scaling gradient norms.
why: Review requested explicit trainability/barren-plateau context without overclaiming.
action: Added mcclean2018barren to references.bib (arxiv 1803.11173); updated Outlook; prose-only (no new experiments).
rationale: Honest scope: benchmark shows no collapse to d=15 in flat setting; not a general no-plateau theorem.
